title: Seb Builds
description: The public build log and project index for Sebastian Mertens: project pages, notes, CLI access, and GitHub Pages deployment for sebmer.com.
body:
Seb Builds is the public build surface behind sebmer.com. It turns active work into project pages, build logs, notes, metadata routes, and agent-readable context files.

The recent local commits show the site becoming more than a starter portfolio. It gained GitHub Pages deployment, public JSON content, a CLI package, the sebmer.com domain wiring, an About page, project narratives, accessibility improvements, and richer logs.

## What changed in the recent commits

- Replaced the starter notes/projects with real public project inventory and build logs.
- Added GitHub Pages deployment, CNAME handling, static export details, legal page, sitemap, robots, and RSS surfaces.
- Prepared a CLI package so the same public content can be inspected from developer workflows.
- Updated GitHub owner references to `sebmer-com`.
- Added the Sebastian profile page, public profile JSON, image layout, and clearer project descriptions.
- Expanded build logs and narratives after the first pass was too thin.

## Why it matters

The site is a memory layer for real work. It is not meant to leak private implementation details; it is meant to keep enough public context that projects do not disappear into private repos and chat transcripts.

## Public boundary

Seb Builds intentionally avoids credentials, private local paths, client-private data, raw exports, and unreleased execution details.
